title: ElevatedEyeProxy: 32 Parallel Acceptors Prevent HANDSHAKE-MISS Burst
desc: ListenAsync spawns 32 concurrent AcceptLoop workers each owning a NamedPipeServerStream. Burst of parallel --sudo ConnectAsync calls are accepted immediately instead of serializing through one listener with an accept-gap.
steps: Root cause: single WaitForConnectionAsync loop created an accept-gap between connections. 26 parallel --sudo calls saturated the 1500ms budget. Fix: ElevatedEyeProxy.ListenAsync spawns AcceptWorkers=32 AcceptLoop tasks, each owning its own NamedPipeServerStream instance ready in WaitForConnectionAsync.

Each AcceptLoop(workerId, ct) independently waits, accepts, dispatches to Task.Run(HandleClient), then immediately loops back to arm a new NamedPipeServerStream. ~32 acceptors are always armed in parallel, absorbing bursts up to 32 simultaneous ConnectAsync without any gap.

If HANDSHAKE-MISS reappears with burst>32, increase AcceptWorkers constant in ElevatedEyeProxy.cs (max=255 per MaxAllowedServerInstances). Also ensures connectedPipe.Dispose() in HandleClient finally to close the prior connection leak.
author: claude
